## Working session summary

Implemented and finalized PR #399: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399

The PR is now ready for review, branch `issue-398-b349f91c312f` is clean, and the latest CI/CD run passed on commit `68454a84`.

What changed:
- Added recursive semantic facets for meanings: `notation`, `annotation`, `denotation`, `connotation`.
- Added seed support and parser/API access for nested `facet` blocks.
- Added multilingual semantic meta seed entries for `en`, `ru`, `hi`, and `zh`.
- Updated `VISION.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, changelog, and issue case-study docs.
- Added regression/unit/source tests covering semantic facet parsing and resolution.

Verification:
- Local checks passed: seed sync/check, focused source tests, semantic facet tests, language coverage check, fmt, clippy, full `cargo test`, and doc tests.
- Investigated the earlier CI failure from run `27072524576`; it failed language test coverage because only `tests/source` changed. Added CI-recognized unit tests, then reran CI successfully in run `27072667121`.

Research sources documented in the PR include Wikidata, Wikimedia dumps, WordNet, SKOS, and OntoLex:
https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Help:Data_model
https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Lexicographical_data/Documentation
https://dumps.wikimedia.org/backup-index.html
https://wordnet.princeton.edu/documentation
https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/
https://www.w3.org/2016/04/ontolex/

My requirements from original task were completely ignored.

_This comment marks the beginning of an AI work session. Please wait for the session to finish, and provide your feedback._

Addressed the 2026-06-06 review feedback in `56a591d1`.

What changed after the review:
- Proposed five alternative meaning data model variants in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/meaning-data-model-alternatives.md`.
- Added word-form semantic facets, not just meaning-level facets.
- Derived `notation -> word_surface` and `denotation -> parent meaning` for every parsed word form, so all 4,416 seed word surfaces expose recursive meaning links at runtime.
- Added explicit lexical meta meanings for word surfaces, lexical forms, lexical senses, part of speech, nouns, and noun phrases.
- Added tests enforcing that all exposed meaning and word-form facets resolve to seed meanings.

Local verification passed: seed sync check, focused seed tests, semantic facet API tests, language coverage check, fmt, file-size check, clippy, full `cargo test`, and doc tests.

## Draft: a self-defining semantic root in Links Notation

Following the thread on #398 that the meaning data structure is "fake" and still leans on hardcoded English, here is a **draft** of a core semantic structure built strictly on [Links Theory](https://github.com/link-foundation/meta-theory): everything is a link, every word is a reference, and the irreducible primitives are defined by their **own structural self-equation** (self-reference with meaningful structure) rather than by English synonyms.

Because everything is already a link, definitions never say "link" just to say it — a reference is named only when it adds real structure.

### Rules for constructing the semantic network

1. **Global, reusable references.** Every reference is defined once, globally, and reused to define others. The network is one shared namespace; a definition anywhere is usable everywhere.
2. **One symbol, one meaning.** A reference has exactly one meaning. If the same symbol is found with two meanings, **split it into two symbols** (`bank-river` vs `bank-money`, `is-identity` vs `is-a-kind-of`). No token is ambiguous for human or machine.
3. **Natural-language, human-readable references**, in a **simple, easily-parsable subset of English**: lowercase words, hyphens to bind a multi-word reference into one symbol (`any-of-reference`, `is-a-kind-of`), the colon for "is a", and a small set of **defined** connectives (`of`, `from`, `to`, `and`) — each itself a link in the network, never undefined prose.
4. **Define a relation by the two links it connects and cannot exist without**, plus its direction. Ask: *which two links does this connect, and could it exist without them?* Example: `child` is the offspring **of** `father` **and** `mother`; remove either parent and `child` has nothing to be. That dependency is the definition.
5. **If no constitutive relation exists, define by supertype** with `is-a-kind-of`. This is the fallback for a category rather than a connection.
6. **`type` is its own type** — `type: type type` — self-defined, with specifics layered on afterward.
7. **Core links are fixed** (`reference`, `link`, `type`); everything else must be compatible with them.

```lino
# root: self-witnessing primitives, each line is what it defines
reference: reference-action link
link: link-action any-of-reference
type: type type

# action and quantity forms; repeatable-from-zero means n in N0
reference-action: makes reference point-at link
link-action: makes link from any-of-reference
any-of-reference: reference repeatable-from-zero
any-of-link: link repeatable-from-zero
repeatable-from-zero: same link-action zero-or-more
makes: link-action that brings into being
point-at: reference-action toward link

# defined connectives, so bodies never use undefined prose
of: belonging from part to whole
from: source end of direction
to: target end of direction
and: this together-with that

# structural primitives, defined by self-equation
not: not (not not)
is-identity: same applied-to same
same: not (not same)
other: not same
single: not other
or-else: choice between this and other
zero: count of empty
one: count of single
more: one and one

# the three specific senses of english is; bare is is never used
is-a-kind-of: subtype toward supertype
is-property: property held-by subject
held-by: from property to subject

# quantity: amount and size kept distinct
quantity: amount or-else size
amount: count of any-of-link
size: extent of single link
count: how-many over distinct reference
extent: how-much over single reference
distinct: other and other
any: quantity not fixed
fixed: same over repeatable-from-zero
```

### More concepts, built on the root above

Still pure links, still no opaque English payload — scaling to structure, number, logic, sets, change, formal and natural language.

```lino
# structure and identity
sequence: order over any-of-reference
order: direction over reference
first: not after-another
last: not before-another
before-another: other toward first
after-another: other toward last
empty: any-of-reference of-size zero
of-size: from any-of-reference to size
part: belonging from smaller to whole
whole: has-part any-of-part
has-part: from whole to part
any-of-part: part repeatable-from-zero
holon: part and whole together
nesting: part within part
position: place of reference over order

# typing and ontology
subtype: is-a-kind-of toward supertype
supertype: has subtype
instance: is-a-kind-of toward type
category: type over any-of-instance
any-of-instance: instance repeatable-from-zero
root-type: type not is-a-kind-of other
defined-by: from concept to any-of-concept
abstract: concept not held-by instance
concrete: concept held-by instance

# number, from the meta-theory N0
number: type of count
successor: from number to one-more number
predecessor: from number to one-less number
one-more: more by one
one-less: less by one
less: not more
sum: from number and number to number
difference: from number and number to less number
product: sum repeated by number
natural-number: from zero by successor
digit: character denotes number

# logic, toward many-valued truth
and-both: this and that hold
or: not (not this and not that)
implies: not (this and not that)
true: holds-for any
false: not true
unknown: not true and not false
holds-for: from truth-value to proposition
proposition: meaning has truth-value
truth-value: quantity over proposition
contradiction: proposition true and false

# set and relation, as ordered-unique sequences per the paper
set: sequence of distinct ordered reference
member: belonging from reference to set
subset: set of member of set
union: from set and set to set
intersection: from set and set to common member
relation: from link to link
direction: order from source to target
source: first of relation
target: last of relation
function: relation from one to one

# time and change, since the network is mutable
time: order over change
change: link-action makes link other
before: less over time
after: more over time
state: link at single time
version: state of link at change
mutation: change over substitution
substitution: link-action replaces pattern by pattern
pattern: matches any-of-link
matches: from pattern to same-shape link

# language layer: words attach to meaning without privileging any tongue
language: from meaning to any-of-expression
any-of-expression: expression repeatable-from-zero
expression: order of any-of-character for meaning
character: is-a-kind-of toward form
form: held-by perceivable
perceivable: property sensed
word: expression stands-for single meaning
stands-for: from expression to meaning
phrase: expression from word and word
denotes: from expression to meaning
connotes: from expression to other meaning
translation: from expression to other-expression of same meaning
synonym: expression denotes same meaning
homonym: expression denotes other meaning
sense: single meaning of expression
lexeme: word of any-of-sense

# grammar of natural language, all as links
part-of-speech: type of word
noun: part-of-speech denotes entity
verb: part-of-speech denotes act
entity: concept held-by bounded
act: link-action change makes
subject: source of verb
object: target of verb
modifier: from word to narrowed meaning
narrowed: smaller meaning toward specific

# formal and programming languages, parsed into the same network
grammar: from language to any-of-rule
any-of-rule: rule repeatable-from-zero
rule: pattern matches expression of language
token: smallest expression
node: part of parse-tree
parse-tree: order of node by rule
cst: parse-tree of any-of-character
ast: parse-tree without trivia
trivia: character not held-by meaningful
mixed-language: expression from expression of other language
region: part of expression in single language

# source and provenance, the #398 grounding requirement
source-of-truth: grounds any-of-link
grounds: from source to grounded link
external-source: source not within network
override: replaces link by marked link
marked: held-by explicit
cached: stores source-response for compare
compare: from link to link by same

# formality, toward relative-meta-logic
formality: quantity from informal to formal
formal: meaning fully defined-by relation
informal: meaning not fully defined-by relation
variable: meaning not fixed
range: any from least to most
clarify: change from informal to more formal
```

### Worked example: applying the construction rules

```lino
# rule 5: not relations between two links, so defined by supertype
person: animal able-to reason
parent: person has-child child
mother: parent held-by female
father: parent held-by male

# rule 4: each relation defined by the two links it connects, with direction
child: offspring of father and mother
has-child: from parent to child
motherhood: from mother to child
fatherhood: from father to child
sibling: from child to other child of same parents
spouse: from person to married person

# rule 2: split an ambiguous symbol into distinct senses
bank-river: land beside water
bank-money: organization keeps money
# never a single bank
```

`child` cannot exist without `father` and `mother` — that pair, as the offspring relation, is its whole definition. `mother` is not a connection between two links, so it falls back to its supertype `parent` plus the specific `held-by female`. And `bank` is split, because one symbol carries only one meaning.

### Status of this draft

- **An example of the core structure, not a finished spec.** A draft, and can be done better.
- It **closes** (every token used reduces to defined ones) but does not claim non-circular grounding: the bottom is a small set of mutually-defining cycles (`count`/`how-many` content, the `not`/`same` self-equations, and `reference`/`link` themselves). The meta-theory explicitly permits self-reference and cycles — this is the intended bottom, the point/line primitives of the system.
- Strongest parts: the **self-equations** (`not: not (not not)`) and the **one-symbol-one-meaning** sense-splitting. The connectives (`of`, `from`, `to`, `and`) are kept but **defined as links**, so bodies never use undefined prose.

### Where this should go next

This can and should be made **even better, and fully proven formally**, with [relative-meta-logic](https://github.com/link-foundation/relative-meta-logic): each definition link becomes a proposition with a truth/typing judgment, the self-equations get checked as genuine fixed points, the ontology roots get dependent types (`(Type: Type Type)`), and the whole semantic root is verified for consistency rather than asserted — a **solid semantic foundation that fully takes advantage of Links Meta Theory**.

Goal: replace the English-keyed `gloss`/`description` seed with this links-network root, so meanings, senses, concepts, and types are recorded as links that define themselves — and translations to any language fall out as `denotes` / `translation` links rather than hardcoded pairs.
